hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of physics-based simulation trajectories—where each trajectory encodes the thermodynamic state evolution resulting from specific sequences of synthesis actions—will yield greater improvements in synthesis planning success rate than prompt-based optimization strategies (such as few-shot tuning), because internalized kinetic and thermodynamic priors will enable the model to predict intermediate phase fractions without invoking the simulator at each reasoning step, thereby reducing compounding errors in multi-phase, multi-temperature challenges like Challenges 2 and 3 where neither A* nor the prompt-tuned LLM currently succeeds.
